perturbation.original_text: 1, GRANT OF LICENSE. (a) MS grants to Recipient a limited, non-exclusive, nontransferable, royalty-free license to use up to five copies of the executable Product code on CPU's residing at Recipient's premises solely to test the compatibility of Recipient's application or other product(s) ("Application") which operate in conjunction with the Product and to evaluate the Product for the purpose of providing feedback thereon to MS. All other rights are reserved to MS. Recipient shall not rent, lease, sell, sublicense, assign, or otherwise transfer the Product, including any accompanying printed materials. Recipient may not reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble the Product except to the extent that this restriction is expressly prohibited by applicable law. MS and its suppliers shall retain title and all ownership rights to the Product.
perturbation.changed_text: 1, GRANT OF LICENSE. (a) MS grants to Recipient a limited, non-exclusive, nontransferable, royalty-free license to use up to five copies of the executable Product code on CPU's residing at Recipient's premises solely to test the compatibility of Recipient's application or other product(s) ("Application") which operate in conjunction with the Product and to evaluate the Product for the purpose of providing feedback thereon to MS. All other rights are reserved to MS. Recipient shall not rent, lease, sell, sublicense, assign, or otherwise transfer the Product, including any accompanying printed materials. Recipient may reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble the Product even if this restriction is not expressly prohibited by applicable law. MS and its suppliers shall retain title and all ownership rights to the Product.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that Recipient may reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble the Product even if this restriction is not expressly prohibited by applicable law. This directly contradicts the original text which stated that Recipient may not reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble the Product except to the extent that this restriction is expressly prohibited by applicable law, creating uncertainty about the limitations regarding reverse engineering.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1, GRANT OF LICENSE. (a) MS grants to Recipient a limited, non-exclusive, nontransferable, royalty-free license to use up to five copies of the executable Product code on CPU's residing at Recipient's premises solely to test the compatibility of Recipient's application or other product(s) ("Application") which operate in conjunction with the Product and to evaluate the Product for the purpose of providing feedback thereon to MS. All other rights are reserved to MS. Recipient shall not rent, lease, sell, sublicense, assign, or otherwise transfer the Product, including any accompanying printed materials. Recipient may reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble the Product even if this restriction is not expressly prohibited by applicable law. MS and its suppliers shall retain title and all ownership rights to the Product.
perturbation.justification: The modified text explicitly allows reverse engineering even if not prohibited by law, whereas the original text only allowed it if expressly prohibited by law. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text, so it still allows reverse engineering even if not prohibited by law. The change creates an in-text contradiction by providing conflicting guidance on the permissibility of reverse engineering, creating uncertainty. This adheres to the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction: Inconsistencies also lead to in-text contradictions when different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties. This creates in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees to provide reasonable feedback to MS, including but not limited to beta reports, usability, bug reports and test results, with respect to the Product testing. Recipient will use reasonable efforts to review and comment on all documentation supplied. All bug reports, test results and other feedback made by Recipient shall be the property of MS and may be used by MS for any purpose. Due to the nature of the development work, MS is not certain as to when errors or discrepancies in the Products may be corrected.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees to provide reasonable feedback to MS, including but not limited to beta reports, usability, bug reports and test results, with respect to the Product testing. Recipient will use reasonable efforts to review and comment on all documentation supplied. All bug reports, test results and other feedback made by Recipient shall be the property of the Recipient and may be used by Recipient for any purpose. Due to the nature of the development work, MS is not certain as to when errors or discrepancies in the Products may be corrected.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that all bug reports, test results and other feedback made by Recipient shall be the property of the Recipient and may be used by Recipient for any purpose. This directly contradicts the original text which stated that All bug reports, test results and other feedback made by Recipient shall be the property of MS and may be used by MS for any purpose. This creates a contradiction about who owns the rights to feedback data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1, GRANT OF LICENSE. (b) Recipient agrees to provide reasonable feedback to MS, including but not limited to beta reports, usability, bug reports and test results, with respect to the Product testing. Recipient will use reasonable efforts to review and comment on all documentation supplied. All bug reports, test results and other feedback made by Recipient shall be the property of the Recipient and may be used by Recipient for any purpose. Due to the nature of the development work, MS is not certain as to when errors or discrepancies in the Products may be corrected.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. Both texts are identical, but that doesn't mean the changed text doesn't create a contradiction. The change in ownership of 'All bug reports, test results and other feedback' from 'MS' in the original text to 'the Recipient' in the changed text introduces a direct and irreconcilable conflict with the contradicted text, which is the changed text itself. This creates a contradiction about who owns the rights to feedback data. According to the Perturbation Explanation this is an in-text contradiction. The modified text alters a definition of ownership in a way that breaks consistency within the contract.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient will have executed appropriate written agreements with its employees sufficient to enable it to comply with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient is not required to have executed any written agreements with its employees to enable it to comply with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that Recipient is not required to have executed any written agreements with its employees to enable it to comply with the terms of this Agreement. This directly contradicts the original text which stated that Recipient will have executed appropriate written agreements with its employees sufficient to enable it to comply with the terms of this Agreement. Now, is is unclear whether an agreement is required or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Recipient may disclose the Product only to its employees who have a need to know in order to accomplish the purposes identified in Section l(a), and such employees' use of the Product shall take place solely at Recipient's site. Recipient is not required to have executed any written agreements with its employees to enable it to comply with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that 'Recipient is not required to have executed any written agreements with its employees to enable it to comply with the terms of this Agreement.' The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, indicating that the contradictory statement now exists within the same section of the document. This creates a direct in-text contradiction because it implies that agreements may or may not be required, creating ambiguity and uncertainty about the Recipient's obligations, and directly in conflict within itself, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: MS further grants to recipient the following non-exclusive, nontransferable, royalty-free rights with respect to the sample and redistributable code listed in the readme.txt file.
perturbation.changed_text: MS further grants to recipient the following exclusive, nontransferable, royalty-free rights with respect to the sample and redistributable code listed in the readme.txt file.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that the rights granted are exclusive, whereas the original states they are non-exclusive. This has implications for whether MS can grant these rights to other parties, causing uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. REDISTRIBUTABLE COMPONENTS. MS further grants to recipient the following exclusive, nontransferable, royalty-free rights with respect to the sample and redistributable code listed in the readme.txt file.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text states that MS grants 'exclusive' rights to the recipient, while the original text stated 'non-exclusive' rights. The Contradicted Text also states that MS grants 'exclusive' rights. By changing the original text from 'non-exclusive' to 'exclusive', it creates a clear contradiction, as MS cannot simultaneously grant both exclusive and non-exclusive rights to the same item. This inconsistency regarding exclusivity directly conflicts with the meaning of 'exclusive' versus 'non-exclusive'. The contradiction exists because the changed text gives rights exclusively, breaking the initial non-exclusivity.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue until terminated by MS in writing at any time, with or without cause. This Agreement will terminate without notice upon the commercial release of the Product.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue for 3 years, regardless of the commercial release of the Product. This Agreement will terminate without notice upon the commercial release of the Product.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction, because we are stating the agreement will last for 3 years, but then still stating that it will terminate without notice upon release of the product.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. TERM OF AGREEMENT. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue for 3 years, regardless of the commercial release of the Product. This Agreement will terminate without notice upon the commercial release of the Product. Upon the termination of this Agreement, Recipient shall promptly return to MS. or certify destruction of, all full or partial copies of the Product and related materials provided by MS. Section 6 shall survive termination or expiration of this Agreement with respect to any information that has not been made public by MS as of the commercial release of the Product.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an inconsistency. The original text allowed termination by MS in writing at any time, but the changed text states the agreement continues for 3 years regardless of the product release, yet also states it terminates upon product release. This creates a direct conflict about the agreement's duration, because it claims it will continue for 3 years *and* terminate upon release, thus the Contradicted Text and Change text contradict each other. This follows the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: There is no charge to Recipient for testing of the Product. MS shall bear all transportation expenses relating to the shipment of the Product to Recipient's place of business and Recipient will pay any return transportation expenses.
perturbation.changed_text: There is a charge of $10,000 to Recipient for testing of the Product. MS shall bear all transportation expenses relating to the shipment of the Product to Recipient's place of business and Recipient will pay any return transportation expenses.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that There is a charge of $10,000 to Recipient for testing of the Product. This directly contradicts the original text which stated that There is no charge to Recipient for testing of the Product.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. COST OF TESTING. There is a charge of $10,000 to Recipient for testing of the Product. MS shall bear all transportation expenses relating to the shipment of the Product to Recipient's place of business and Recipient will pay any return transportation expenses.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a charge of $10,000 for testing the product, directly contradicting the original text, which stated that there would be no charge. This creates a clear inconsistency with the contradicted text, which also states there is a charge of $10,000, as it highlights the contradiction within the agreement regarding the cost of testing.
